text:
Market today: AI demand narrative is carrying tech higher - CNBC flags AI fueling portfolios while oil posts its biggest monthly decline. That's a clean tailwind for your chip/tech concentration. The macro overhang is the Fed vs. yen dynamic (Japan spent $74B defending yen), which means rate-sensitive volatility can spike fast. Your leveraged names feel it hardest.

Your holdings: All scanner statuses say HOLD or NEW - nothing flashing red. SOXL (+4.8%) is 3x leveraged semiconductors - great when chips run, brutal when they don't, and decay eats you if it chops. SPAL is brand-new, very volatile, and moves fast - margin amplifies any drop hard. QQQI (+0.67%) is barely moving; low concern but watch it drift lower.

Watch: NVDA and ALAB - both in scanner setups and directly tied to today's AI demand headline. If AI sentiment stays bid, these are your tells for whether the move has legs.

Bottom line: Tailwind is real today, but you're leveraged and concentrated - protect your stops, don't chase.
plays: SOXL: Respect the $200 stop - 3x leverage means a chip pullback hits you triple; no reason to tighten yet but don't move the stop up recklessly while it's chopping.
SPAL: Very new, very fast - keep position small and honor the $21.38 stop hard; margin on a leveraged ETF that gaps is a painful combo.
No new buys today - NVDA scanner setup (entry 198.39-198.62, stop 197.35) is only grade C conviction; wait for ALAB to pull into 482-482.54 with volume before considering.
